rulings.Case Title: JOSE CERON ET AL VS. SALLY LIU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday January 18, 2019 line 6. CROSS DEFENDANT BRIAN RANGEL DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OVERRULED. According to Cross-Defendant's Demurrer Rangel "demurs to the First Amended Complaint." However, points and authorities in support of the demurrer do not address the fourth cause of action asserted against defendant Rangel. Rangel did not separately demur to each cause of action. Cross-Complainant's counsel is ordered to comply with CRC 2.104 and 2.105 in future. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE CERON ET AL VS. SALLY LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday January 18, 2019 line 6. CROSS DEFENDANT BRIAN RANGEL MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT GRANTED. Request for Judicial notice is granted. No opposition filed to the request for judicial notice and Cross-Complainant relies on the contents of the settlement agreement it is opposition. Motion is GRANTED without leave to amend as to the 13th and 14th causes of action. Cross-Complainant concedes that these causes of action are barred as to the sole moving cross-defendant. Motion to Strike references to Rangel from the 4th cause of action is GRANTED with leave to amend. Cross-Complainant concedes that cause of action for ejectment "is appropriate where the landlord seeks to recover possession from a tenant who has breached the lease." (Opposition 2: 19-20). However, no facts are alleged as to Ragel breaching any lease he is a party to. Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend to allege facts (not conclusions) supporting malice, oppression or fraud by Rangel. Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to claim for attorney's fees, e.g. statute or binding contract. Cross-Complainant's counsel is ordered to comply with CRC 2.104 and 2.105 in future. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BRENT MARSH VS. MICHAEL CAMPESINO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday January 18, 2019 line 9. DEFENDANT MICHAEL CAMPESINO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OFF CALENDAR, First Amended Complaint on file. The Court notes that First Amended Complaint was filed in violation of CCP 472 and without leave of Court. Hearing Required to address why an OSC Re: Contempt shall not issue for knowing violation of CCP 472(a). (First Amended Complaint filed on January 9, 2019; e-mail addressing CCP 472 from Defendant's counsel sent on January 7, 2019 and responded to by Plaintiff's counsel on January 8, 2019). = (501/CFH)
